summary: Deprecated audit trail for the old composite toggle. The validated Offline Files policy and the broader service, task, and binary subrecords are tracked separately.

setting.name: Offline Files Composite Toggle
setting.casual_explanation: Offline Files keeps copies of network files for use while disconnected. This top-level toggle tries to shut down the feature more aggressively than the official policy alone.
setting.why_people_change_it: People do this when they want Offline Files fully out of the way, including its supporting services, tasks, and Sync Center entry points.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: The official policy part is strong and documented, but the rest of the bundle goes beyond that policy and changes more of the feature's plumbing.
setting.professional_notes: privacy.disable-offline-files wraps privacy.disable-offline-files.policy plus service, task, and file-rename sub-tweaks. The policy subrecord is validated. The other subrecords remain review-required, so the parent composite should also remain review-required.

targets.path: Composite: Offline Files policy + services + tasks + Sync Center binary

allowed_values.label: Windows default
allowed_values.meaning: Use the normal Offline Files baseline or only the separately documented policy control.

allowed_values.label: Current app bundle
allowed_values.meaning: Apply the broader policy, service, task, and binary changes exposed by the app.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app exposes a composite that includes one validated policy control plus several broader implementation-level sub-tweaks.

writes.path: Composite: Offline Files policy + services + tasks + Sync Center binary

windows_defaults.label: Offline Files baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows client and server systems with Offline Files support

states.rationale: The parent bundle should not outrun the validated policy subrecord.

recommended_profiles.label: Policy-only baseline
intended_for: Anyone who wants the official control first
avoid_for: People who specifically need the broader app bundle for research

states.rationale: Prefer the validated policy-only understanding until the broader bundle is settled.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the official feature control without extra plumbing changes.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are intentionally documenting or testing the broader app bundle.
tradeoffs: Stays close to Microsoft's documented policy.
Does not aggressively strip services or UI entry points.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app bundle

states.rationale: Track the broader bundle the app exposes today.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You need to document the full current product behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want only the official feature policy.
tradeoffs: More aggressive shutdown of the feature area.
Less aligned with the single official policy control.

evidence.title: Existing Offline Files policy record
evidence.summary: The core NetCache policy mapping is already validated and documents the official feature surface.

evidence.title: Current privacy provider composite
evidence.summary: The provider exposes privacy.disable-offline-files as a composite of the policy, service, task, and binary sub-tweaks.

decision.why: The parent toggle is broader than the official policy surface and includes subrecords that still need review.
blocking_issues: The composite includes service, task, and binary changes that are not part of the validated Offline Files policy surface.
